description:
As advocates fighting against coercion in the dog world, uncritically celebrating military and police dogs creates a glaring inconsistency - it treats sentient beings as tools for colonization and suppression.

We cannot oppose coercion while celebrating the weaponization of dogs for police and military use. These systems are built on coercion, control, and carceral force against humans too. 

Modern trainers understand that coercion is wrong. It does not become ethical just because the dogs wer
title: Honoring Military Working Dogs
